# Research: speckit-workflow-resilience
<!-- Generated by /speckit.plan Phase 0; rewritten for revision 3 (scope A only) -->
<!-- Date: 2026-07-16 -->

Truth Map for feature **A (resilience)**. Every claim carries a `file:line` citation or is the output of
a spike recorded here. After reading this a downstream agent should not need to re-explore the repo.

> **Scoped to A deliberately.** This file was rewritten rather than annotated. The previous revision
> carried multi-repo spikes and a symbol map for features B and C, and referenced FR numbers that now
> mean *different things* in a 13-FR spec — `FR-006`, `FR-007` and `FR-008` were all live, in-range, and
> silently wrong. A stale Truth Map that a reader is told to trust over the repo is worse than none, and
> an "some numbers are superseded" notice cannot protect an in-range collision: the reader greps, gets a
> confident answer, and never sees a disagreement. B's evidence now lives in **#29**, C's in **#30**.

## Affected Modules

| Path | Role | Key Symbols (file:line) |
|------|------|-------------------------|
| `workflows/speckit-workflow.js` | The whole feature. 421 lines. | `implementAndVerify:250`, batch collector `:320`, seq loop `:325`, halt `:329`, gate `:342`, gate check `:358`, tasks.md write `:376`, clean return `:414` |
| `tests/workflow.test.js` | **NEW** — `node --test` harness | n/a |
| `tests/smoke.sh` | 450 lines, 38 checks, shell only. Header comments encode the `pipefail`+`grep -q` trap | version guard `:69-94`, component loop `:107` |
| `.github/workflows/smoke.yml` | CI. `ubuntu-latest`, no runtime setup step | `run: tests/smoke.sh:24` |

## Relevant Symbols

- `:250` — `implementAndVerify(task)`: the unit of work. Returns a result object on **every** path; only
  an `agent()` throw escapes it.
- `:256` — `if (!impl) return {accepted:false, reason:'implementer died or was skipped'}`. **The null path
  is already handled correctly.** Only the throw path is not. This is why the fix routes throws into an
  existing contract rather than inventing one — the single most load-bearing fact in this feature.
- `:320` — `(await parallel(...)).filter(Boolean)` — **the silent drop** (Spike 2).
- `:325` — `for (const t of seq) phaseResults.push(await implementAndVerify(t))` — **the run-killer**
  (Spike 2). No `parallel` wrapper, so a throw propagates out of the script.
- `:329` — the halt, driven by `phaseResults.filter(r => !r.accepted)`. A dropped task is not in
  `phaseResults` at all, so it cannot halt the phase.
- `:353` — "If the project configures no gate at all, that is an ABSENCE of a gate, not a failure —
  report passed:true." **Precedent: absence ≠ failure.** Note it was written for a project that *has* no
  gate; see Open Questions for what else reaches it.
- `:358` — `if (gate && !gate.passed)` — **a null gate silently passes** (Spike 5).
- `:376` — `await agent(...)` with the result **discarded**, no schema. A null means tasks.md is never
  ticked while the run reports clean.
- `:418` — `total: results.length`.
- `:293-297` — the file **refuses to trust the model-written `[P]` marker** and re-derives safety from
  file lists. The governing precedent for distrusting any model-authored field, including task ids.
- `:104` — "Redundant verifiers find the same thing; diverse ones catch failure modes the others are
  blind to." Why a shrunken quorum (#28) matters more than a count suggests.

## Existing Patterns & Conventions

- **Prompts carry the contract, not the code.** Verified: `ctx.projectRoot` appears only inside template
  strings (`:137`, `:170`, `:343-345`, `:392`) with zero control-flow uses.
- **Schemas are the validation layer** — `agent({schema})` forces a StructuredOutput call; the script
  never parses free text.
- **`parallel()` never rejects** — "a thunk that throws … resolves to `null`" (Workflow tool contract,
  quoted 2026-07-16). This is *why* `:320` drops silently and `:325` behaves oppositely.
- **Comments state the trap and why the obvious fix is wrong** (`:13-28`, `:104`, `:293`, `:374`).
- **Shell guards capture-then-match**, never `producer | grep -q` (`smoke.sh` header; constitution 3).

## Spikes (executed 2026-07-16)

### Spike 1 — the `new Function` harness is viable ✅
`.replace('export const meta','const meta')` + `new Function(...)` executes the **shipped file
unmodified**. Top-level `return` (`:241`, `:337`, `:414`) is illegal in an ES module but legal in a
function body — which is why the wrap is both necessary and sufficient. Drove a 2-task graph:
`{"completed":2,"total":2}`.

### Spike 2 — both failure modes reproduced ✅ (US1, US2)
With `impl:T001` throwing `"completed without calling StructuredOutput"`:

| Path | Line | Observed |
|------|------|----------|
| `[P]` | `:320` | **T001 vanished.** Ledger `[{"id":"T002","accepted":true}]`. Return `{"completed":1,"total":1}` |
| sequential | `:325` | **Whole run died**, unhandled throw. All prior agents lost. |

`total` excludes the dropped task too, so the run reports **1/1 — a perfect score**.
**Same root cause, opposite symptoms: the sequential path screams, the parallel path lies.**

### Spike 3 — fan-out amplitude, measured ✅ (→ #29)
2 tasks ⇒ 12 agents, peak 6, of which **3 run a full suite**. Detail in #29.

### Spike 4 — this repo has no detectable stack ✅
`detect-stack` → empty. `list-config-files` → "No config files found". No `package.json`. The suite is
`tests/smoke.sh` (38 checks). Consequences: (a) `node --test` needs no `package.json` and CI ships node,
confirming CLR-001 at zero install cost; (b) **the loader's `testCommand` detection returns `""` for this
very repo, which plainly has tests** — see Open Questions.

### Spike 5 — a null gate silently passes ✅ (US4)
Both phase gates dying:
```
TODAY:  gate throws  -> run DIES loudly -> phase 2 never runs
AFTER routing the gate through agentTyped, WITHOUT the :358 fix:
        gate -> null -> phase 2 runs anyway -> {"completed":2,"total":2}
```
A clean bill of health for a run where **no gate confirmed anything**. Quantified independently: 6 gate
invocations, 0 confirmations. This is `:320`'s silent drop reintroduced one function away, *by the fix
for `:320`* — which is why Sketches 1+2+3+4 are one commit.

### Spike 6 — a non-Error throw kills the error handler ✅ (US1)
```
throw new Error()  -> returned null   ✅
throw a string     -> returned null   ✅
throw null         -> TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'message')
throw undefined    -> TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'message')
```
`e.message` inside the `catch` throws. The handler becomes the failure, killing the sequential run — the
exact US1 scenario. The `[P]` path survives via Sketch 2, **inverting this feature's own thesis**. The
harness's throw *type* is undocumented, so reachability is unknown; the fix costs two characters.

### Spike 7 — the ledger's identity must not be the model's ✅ (US8)
`P1=[T001], P2=[T001]`, halt after P1 with T001 accepted:
```
keyed by task.id  -> {"total":2,"notAttempted":[]}       balance 1 vs total 2  <<< A TASK VANISHES
keyed by _key     -> {"total":2,"notAttempted":["T001"]} balance 2 vs total 2  OK
```
Deriving `notAttempted` from the task graph does not help when the derivation key is one the model can
forge. `:293-297` already distrusts model-authored markers; ids earn the same distrust.

## Open Questions

**All four `[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]` markers were resolved at the clarify gate (CLR-001..004).** Spike 1
then *proved* CLR-001 rather than assuming it. What remains is owned elsewhere, and every item below is
**filed** — a defect recorded only in prose in a feature's directory is how #27 sat unfiled while the
file around it went stale:

- **#27** — `speckit-helper.sh:6` resolves artifacts by branch name and returns `NO_SPEC` at **exit 0** on
  mismatch. Constitution principle 5. Bit this very run.
- **#28** — `:269` filters null verdicts, so 2-of-3 nulls accepts a task on a single lens.
- **#29** — feature B (fan-out amplitude), carrying Spike 3 and every measured concurrency finding.
- **#30** — feature C (multi-repo), carrying the four open design questions.

**Two further instances of A's own class — "a value meaning *we do not know* treated as *everything is
fine*" — found while reviewing A, pre-existing and untouched by it.** A audits exactly one instance of
that class (null returns from `agent()`); these are the others, and they are not A's to fix:

- **`testCommand: ""` conflates "no tests exist" with "detection failed."** `:40` declares it "empty if
  none detected"; `:246` warns and proceeds; `:348` interpolates it into the gate prompt, so `""` tells
  the gate agent "Full test suite — must be green" with no command; `:353` then tells that agent a
  project with no gate should report `passed:true`. **Spike 4 proves this repo is that case.** Run the
  workflow on the framework itself and the whole adversarial apparatus evaporates, reporting `N/N` clean.
  `:353`'s precedent was written for a project that *has* no tests; nothing distinguishes that from a
  loader that failed.
- **The Iron Law's mechanical evidence is collected and discarded.** `IMPL_SCHEMA:71` requires only
  `['id','succeeded','summary']`, so `redConfirmed`/`greenConfirmed` are optional. Grepped every use:
  `:79-80` (schema), `:159` (prompt), `:176` (interpolated into `verifyPrompt`). **Never read by any
  code — zero conditionals.** `:278` accepts on `refutations.length === 0 && verdicts.length > 0` and
  never consults them. An implementer reporting `redConfirmed: false` — admitting the test never failed
  first — is accepted identically to one that confirmed the cycle. The only mitigation is that a verifier
  *might* refute on the prose, which is model judgment — against a file whose founding thesis (`:14-21`)
  is "a script guarantees the barrier. A model can talk itself into skipping ahead."
